terms.definition: A small word used before a noun to tell whether we mean something specific or something general. The articles in English are 'a', 'an', and 'the'.
terms.exampleUsage: In the sentence 'I read a book', the word 'a' is an article.

terms.definition: The letters A, E, I, O, U are called vowels. They make open sounds when you say them. We use 'an' before words that start with a vowel sound.
terms.exampleUsage: The word 'apple' starts with the vowel 'a', so we say 'an apple'.

terms.definition: All the letters of the alphabet except A, E, I, O, U are consonants. Examples: B, C, D, F, G. We use 'a' before words that start with a consonant sound.
terms.exampleUsage: The word 'book' starts with the consonant 'b', so we say 'a book'.

terms.definition: A shop where books are sold. You can visit a bookshop to choose and buy books that you want to read.
terms.exampleUsage: We went to the bookshop to buy a new storybook.

terms.definition: The ability to form pictures, ideas, and stories in your mind. Books help us use our imagination to see things that are not in front of us.
terms.exampleUsage: Reading books filled her imagination with wonderful adventures.

terms.definition: Something very valuable and precious. Books are called treasures because the knowledge and stories inside them are very valuable.
terms.exampleUsage: The old library was a treasure full of wonderful books.

terms.definition: An exciting or unusual experience. When you read a book, you go on an adventure in your mind.
terms.exampleUsage: The storybook was full of adventure and excitement.

terms.definition: A place where many books are kept for people to borrow and read. A library is different from a bookshop because you borrow books for free instead of buying them.
terms.exampleUsage: She borrowed a fairy tale from the school library.

terms.definition: A person who writes a book. The author creates the stories, characters, and ideas you find in a book.
terms.exampleUsage: The author of this storybook has written many books for children.

terms.definition: Clearly defined or particular; one certain thing. We use 'the' before a noun when talking about something specific.
terms.exampleUsage: Please give me the red book -- I want that specific one.
